policy.purpose: Executable intake guard for Phase 4 roadmap work.
policy.rule: A Phase 4 task cannot move to ready, in_progress, or done without complete intake metadata.

tasks.plan_anchor: Phase 4 / Realistic high-impact execution order / Acceptance
tasks.buyer_user_value: Keeps Phase 4 execution focused on buyer-relevant runtime action control instead of low-yield detector or positioning drift.
tasks.threat_model: Unscoped roadmap work can add unwired detectors, unsupported benchmark claims, or public copy that outruns evidence.
required_evidence: machine-readable intake ledger
validator wired into Makefile
focused validator regression tests
internal PLAN and AUDIT updates
tasks.claim_boundary: Internal execution governance only; this does not create benchmark or deployment claims.

tasks.buyer_user_value: Supplies defensible comparison evidence for procurement and security review once licence-reviewed third-party artefacts are locally available.
tasks.threat_model: Benchmark overclaiming, copied upstream examples, mixed authored/external metrics, and unreviewed third-party data import.
required_evidence: licence-reviewed local JSONL exports
SOURCE_LEDGER import_allowed=true for each populated surface
partition validator pass
isolated benchmark evidence with host-load context
Markdown and JSON evidence reports with external metrics separated
tasks.claim_boundary: No public external or comparative benchmark claim until reviewed external artefacts are loaded and an isolated run is recorded.
open_blockers: No licence-reviewed local external benchmark JSONL artefacts are present in benchmarks/external_sources/.
Current evidence remains local regression evidence with external n=0.

tasks.buyer_user_value: Lets operators treat the governance posture as reviewed, content-addressed policy: every deployment profile change has a stable digest, a parent lineage for rollback, and a structured field-level diff, and a running profile can be checked for drift against the approved revision before it governs live actions.
tasks.threat_model: Silent or unreviewed policy edits relax thresholds, audit, approval, or the capability envelope without a trace; a deployed profile drifts away from the approved one; a relaxed posture cannot be reverted because no prior revision is recorded.
required_evidence: content-addressed PolicyRevision with sha256 over canonical profile payload matching the audit-chain digest convention
structured field-level diff between two revisions
drift detection between an approved revision and a live profile
100% line and branch coverage with multi-angle regression tests
mypy --strict clean and ruff clean
tasks.claim_boundary: Internal governance primitive only: revision/diff/drift over deployment profiles. No public benchmark, accuracy, or deployment claim; PR-review, A/B, and rollback orchestration are later increments.

tasks.buyer_user_value: Gives operators an append-only history of governance-posture changes with a tamper-evident parent lineage and audit-preserving rollback, so a relaxed posture can be reverted without erasing the record of who relaxed it and why.
tasks.threat_model: Policy history is forked, reordered, or silently rewritten; a rollback erases the relaxation it reverts; a tampered parent digest hides an unauthorised posture change.
required_evidence: PolicyHistory.append rejects orphaned first revisions and parent digests that do not match the current head
rollback appends the restored posture rather than deleting history
lookup by content address
100% line and branch coverage with multi-angle regression tests
mypy --strict clean and ruff clean
tasks.claim_boundary: Internal governance primitive only: append-only revision history and rollback over deployment profiles. No public benchmark, accuracy, or deployment claim; review workflow, A/B, and drift alarms are increment 3.

tasks.buyer_user_value: Makes a governance-posture change a reviewed, approval-bound event: a proposed posture only takes effect after a second identity approves it, so no operator can silently relax thresholds, audit, approval, or the capability envelope, and every change records who proposed it, who reviewed it, and when.
tasks.threat_model: An operator self-approves a relaxation; a change is committed against a base that moved since review; a no-op or duplicate proposal clutters the queue; a denied change is treated as applied.
required_evidence: PolicyChangeReview gates commits behind propose + approve
self-approval rejected
stale proposals rejected when the head moved since propose
no-op and duplicate-pending proposals rejected
denial does not commit to the history
100% line and branch coverage with multi-angle regression tests
mypy --strict clean and ruff clean
tasks.claim_boundary: Internal governance primitive only: approval-gated review over deployment-profile changes. No public benchmark, accuracy, or deployment claim; A/B exposure and audit-sink drift alarms are increment 3b.

tasks.buyer_user_value: Detects when the posture a deployment is actually running has drifted from the approved revision through an out-of-band edit, and records the divergence to the operator's audit trail, so an unauthorised relaxation is caught and evidenced instead of silently governing live actions.
tasks.threat_model: A live profile is edited out of band to relax thresholds, audit, approval, or the capability envelope without going through review; the drift governs actions undetected and unrecorded.
required_evidence: PolicyDriftMonitor.check returns and emits a PolicyDriftEvent only when the live profile diverges from the approved revision
event carries approved and live content addresses, drifted fields, and detection time
sink is pluggable so it wires to a tamper-evident audit trail
100% line and branch coverage with multi-angle regression tests
mypy --strict clean and ruff clean
tasks.claim_boundary: Internal governance primitive only: drift detection over deployment profiles with a pluggable event sink. No public benchmark, accuracy, or deployment claim; A/B posture exposure is increment 3c.

tasks.buyer_user_value: Lets an operator see, before approving a posture change, exactly how the candidate posture would have decided a representative request set versus the approved one.
tasks.threat_model: A posture change is approved without understanding its decision impact: a relaxed candidate silently turns blocks into allows on dangerous actions, or an over-tightened one floods human approval and gets bypassed.
required_evidence: PostureExposure replays the same detector signals under the baseline and candidate fusion policies
report exposes per-case outcome changes and transition counts
capability-policy changes are replayed, not only detector-threshold changes
100% line and branch coverage with multi-angle regression tests
mypy --strict clean and ruff clean
tasks.claim_boundary: Internal governance primitive only: A/B decision-delta of two deployment postures over a supplied case set. No public benchmark, accuracy, or deployment claim.

tasks.plan_anchor: Phase 4 / Guardrail-as-Code policy lifecycle / Operator wiring
tasks.buyer_user_value: Turns the Guardrail-as-Code primitives into one operator command set: an operator proposes a candidate posture, A/B-replays a case corpus, has a second reviewer approve or deny it, and checks a live deployment for drift.
tasks.threat_model: The lifecycle existed only as in-memory library primitives, so it could not actually gate a deployment or survive across invocations.
required_evidence: PolicyGovernance orchestrates propose, expose, approve, deny, rollback, and drift over an approval-gated review
store.py serialises the revision lineage and proposals to JSON and rejects tampered ledgers on load
corpus.py loads an A/B exposure case corpus from JSON
director-class-policy CLI exposes the lifecycle and leaves the ledger untouched on errors
100% line and branch coverage on new modules; mypy --strict and ruff clean
tasks.claim_boundary: Internal governance workflow and CLI over the policy primitives; deterministic, no model or network dependency. No public benchmark or accuracy claim.
